title: HYP-055 PARTIAL CONFIRMATION: USDJPY MVD=-1 significant (p=0.012), USDCAD marginal (p=0.175)
body: Formal permutation test (N=10,000) on macro_vs_direction filter for USDJPY and USDCAD (4-pair live portfolio, 505 trades 2015-2024). USDJPY: MVD=-1 Sharpe=0.293, MVD=+1 Sharpe=-0.114, delta=+0.406, p=0.0117 — SIGNIFICANT. USDCAD: MVD=-1 Sharpe=0.158, MVD=+1 Sharpe=-0.026, delta=+0.184, p=0.1754 — NOT SIGNIFICANT. Combined USDJPY+USDCAD full-period: p=0.0117 (significant). OOS-only (2023-2024): p=0.2106 — NOT SIGNIFICANT, but n=15 MVD=-1 trades is severely underpowered. 2024 specifically: MVD=-1 trades Sharpe=-0.115 vs baseline 0.091 — the filter HURTS in 2024. 3-gate stack (widening + rate level + HYP-055 MVD filter): IS=0.316, OOS=0.316 vs 2-gate IS=0.218, OOS=0.315. Adding HYP-055 improves IS by +0.098 but OOS unchanged. Recommendation: DO NOT implement as hard gate. Consider as USDJPY-only size modifier (reduce size when MVD=+1 for USDJPY). USDCAD needs more data — 2026+ live data required for confirmation.

title: HYP-056 CANDIDATE: Counter-momentum entries outperform across portfolio (OOS p=0.039)
body: Momentum gate analysis (RQ-REST-005): momentum_63d field measures 63-day trailing price return. Alignment = trade direction * momentum_63d > 0. Counter = trade direction * momentum_63d < 0. Full portfolio results: Counter Sharpe=0.163 (n=272) vs Aligned Sharpe=0.088 (n=233), full-period p=0.185 (not significant). OOS (2023-2024): Counter Sharpe=0.343 (n=58) vs Aligned Sharpe=0.044 (n=67), OOS p=0.039 — SIGNIFICANT. Per-pair: EURUSD counter vs aligned p=0.037 (significant), USDCAD p=0.128 (not significant), USDJPY REVERSED — aligned beats counter (p=0.877). EURUSD OOS specifically: counter=0.480 vs aligned=-0.324. Economic rationale: the system is entering before price follows the macro signal (Innovator→Imitator→Loser tenet). USDJPY exception: aligned-momentum outperforms, consistent with USDJPY being a mean-reversion carry pair where price momentum reinforces carry direction. Status: CANDIDATE — full-period permutation not significant (p=0.185), OOS significance may be 2023-driven. Needs additional 2025-2026 data to confirm.

title: 3-gate stack: widening + rate level + momentum counter gives OOS Sharpe 0.499 (from 0.315 on 2-gate)
body: Adding momentum-counter filter to the confirmed 2-gate stack (widening + rate level): 2-gate: IS=0.218 (n=143), OOS=0.315 (n=43). 3-gate (+ momentum counter): IS=0.272 (n=82), OOS=0.499 (n=21). IS < OOS: excellent signal. Year breakdown: 2021=0.018, 2022=0.293, 2023=0.736, 2024=0.233. Crucially: 2024 improves to 0.233 from 0.001 baseline — the momentum counter filter does NOT hurt the problem year (unlike HYP-055 which hurts 2024). Permutation test within 2-gate universe (counter vs aligned): full-period delta=0.184, p=0.065 (marginal). OOS-only permutation: delta=0.371, p=0.101 (not significant at 0.05 — thin n=21 OOS trades). The signal is directionally consistent and economically coherent but underpowered. With ~10 OOS counter-momentum trades per year, another 2-3 years of live data would resolve. IMPLEMENTATION NOTE: This filter cannot be applied perfectly ex-ante because momentum_63d must be computed from price history. Signal_engine.py already has price access — would require adding a 63d return computation before signal gate check.

title: Architecture insight: both HYP-055 and HYP-056 point to early-entry timing as the core edge source
body: REST-006 revealed two distinct but related effects: HYP-055 (macro contrarian: MVD=-1 better for USDJPY/USDCAD) and HYP-056 (momentum counter: price hasn't moved yet in the trade direction). Both are operationalizations of the same tenet: INNOVATOR beats IMITATOR. Enter before the consensus move is priced in. The difference: MVD measures whether the MACRO SIGNAL agrees with trade direction. Momentum_63d measures whether PRICE has moved in trade direction already. For USDJPY: best when macro is contrarian (early macro signal) but price is aligned (carry already visible in price). For EURUSD: best when price is counter-momentum (fundamental signal not yet reflected in price). These pair-level differences suggest distinct market microstructures: USDJPY carry trades are driven by sustained rate differentials visible to everyone but slow to close (carry). EURUSD macro swings are driven by rate surprises that price catches up to after entry. Implementation hierarchy: First confirm gates statistically (more data needed). Near-term: implement HYP-054 + HYP-052c (already confirmed). Next cycle: track momentum_63d at entry in decision_logger for EURUSD trades, so HYP-056 can be validated live.

title: Forex regime-fragility is seated in the EXIT mechanism, not entry selection
body: Decomposed the costed 4-pair v015/HYP-045 trade log (logs/forex_backtest_trades.json, 318 trades 2015-2022, EURUSD/GBPUSD/USDJPY/AUDUSD) net-of-cost by exit_reason. The entire edge comes from 'time' exits: n=149, WR=69.8%, meanR=+0.953, sumR=+141.9R. Every other exit mechanism is net-negative or marginal: trailing_stop n=97 WR=25.8% meanR=-0.505 sumR=-49.0R; reversal n=64 WR=23.4% meanR=+0.279 sumR=+17.8R; hard stop n=8 meanR=-1.929 sumR=-15.4R. The year x exit interaction shows trailing_stop is POSITIVE only in the two trending/crisis years (2015 +3.5R, 2020 +4.2R) and bleeds -7 to -19R in every flat-rate year (2016-2019, 2022; 2022 trailing alone = -18.7R on 25 trades). The 'regime fragility' CLAUDE.md flags is therefore mechanical: in non-trending regimes the trailing stop converts would-be time-exit holds into -0.5R losers. Lever is exit-rule design, not a new entry gate.

title: Trailing-stop drag is robust across all 4 pairs and statistically reliable
body: Robustness battery on the trailing_stop drag. Per-pair: trailing_stop is net-negative for EVERY pair (AUDUSD -20.8R/-0.548 meanR, EURUSD -14.3R/-0.680, GBPUSD -7.3R/-1.048, USDJPY -6.5R/-0.211) while 'time' exits are strongly positive for every pair (+31.7 to +41.4R). Bootstrap (10k) on trailing_stop meanR: 95% CI [-0.795, -0.186], P(meanR<0)=0.999. Permutation test (10k) time vs trailing meanR: observed delta +1.457R, p<0.0001. This independently REPLICATES the older RQ-010/HYP-019 forensic observation (trailing 17.1% WR vs time 77.3% WR) on a fresh, costed, 4-pair dataset — upgrading it from single-run forensics to a robust structural fact. Upper-bound illustration: if every trailing_stop exit had returned 0R, total net would rise +49R (+51%); this is NOT achievable ex-ante (you cannot pre-select which trades the trailing stop produces) and is reported only to size the opportunity in the exit-rule lever.

title: RQ-010 (fixed TP/SL vs trailing) is the highest-leverage unexecuted task — escalate
body: The exit-mechanism decomposition makes RQ-010 the single highest-leverage open item. RQ-010 (replace 1.25x ATR trailing with fixed TP/SL sweep) has sat in status ERROR/DRY-RUN since 2026-05-18 and was never actually executed. FIND-REST-012-a/b now give it a strong, fresh prior: the trailing stop is the mechanical seat of the regime fragility. The honest test requires re-simulating exits on price history (yfinance blocked in sandbox; needs cached parquet or a network-enabled run) and, because it changes live exit logic, requires Colin approval (NON-NEGOTIABLE #4 + live-pipeline change). Complementary to HYP-058: last cycle's signed-velocity overlay cut maxDD 60% by de-sizing flat regimes; this finding says the SAME flat regimes are where the trailing stop bleeds — two independent angles on one regime. Recommend running RQ-010 properly next network-enabled cycle, then a Colin policy decision.

title: HYP-058 velocity overlay and HYP-059 trailing-stop drag are ORTHOGONAL, not redundant
body: Tested the prior-cycle conjecture (HYP-059 verdict said the signed-velocity defense overlay and the trailing-stop drag target 'the same flat regimes'). Tagged all 318 costed 4-pair trades with the HYP-058 causal-percentile velocity bucket at entry (LOW 0.25x / MID 0.5x / HIGH 1.0x, expanding window min 252 obs, RQ-REST-011 parameterization). The overlay applies a near-identical mean multiplier to every exit type: time 0.883, trailing_stop 0.871, reversal 0.879 (signed 90d). The throttle on the -49R trailing-stop cluster is statistically indistinguishable from the throttle on the +142R time-winner cluster: diff=-0.011, permutation p=0.387 (one-sided, trailing throttled MORE). The |abs| 90d variant agrees: diff=-0.018, p=0.318. LOW-bucket share is ~uniform across exits (time 0.07, trailing 0.10). CONCLUSION: the velocity overlay de-sizes whole low-velocity calendar windows (winners and losers alike) and does NOT preferentially suppress the trailing-stop losses, which are spread across velocity regimes (77/97 land in the HIGH=full-size bucket). The two levers operate on different axes -> NOT redundant. The exit-rule fix (RQ-010/RQ-REST-013) cannot be substituted by the size overlay; they stack.

title: On the entry-selected 2015-2022 log the velocity overlay is rarely active and costs sumR; its value is variance-only
body: Sizing of the redundancy result: under causal expanding percentiles, 255/318 (80%) of 2015-2022 trades fall in the HIGH=full-size bucket because portfolio rate-spread velocity trended upward over the sample, so the overlay seldom fires on this log. When applied, it lowers total return: base +95.3R -> gated +86.9R (Delta -8.4R, signed; -5.4R abs) because it shrinks the dominant +142R time-winners (-10.5R) more in absolute terms than it trims the -49R trailing losers (+2.5R). The trailing cluster supplies a NEGATIVE share (-0.30) of the overlay's total R change. This is fully consistent with HYP-058's own result (Sharpe delta slightly negative; the only win was the signed variant's ~60% maxDD reduction). Net: the overlay's sole justification is drawdown/variance control achieved by de-sizing whole flat windows -- it is NOT a return enhancer and NOT a mechanism for neutralizing the trailing-stop bleed. Caveat: log ends 2022; the 2024 bad year (where an active overlay might matter more) is not testable in sandbox (yfinance blocked).

title: Rate-spread |velocity| size gate REJECTED on canonical costed v003 log — reduces portfolio Sharpe at every causal lookback and even under look-ahead
body: RQ-REST-011 ran the proposed 90d rolling rate-spread |velocity| position-size throttle (velocity>p50->1.0x, p25-p50->0.5x, <p25->0.25x) over the canonical v003 4-pair costed log (n=276 trades, 2015-2022; baseline portfolio Sharpe 0.6702 reproduced exactly from v003 metadata). PRIMARY causal |vel| 90d: gated Sharpe 0.6354 (dSharpe -0.035), maxDD unchanged -0.0967. 60d -0.028, 120d -0.030 — every causal lookback is NEGATIVE. The full-sample LOOK-AHEAD variant (which is allowed to cheat) is still -0.0096, so there is no recoverable benefit even with hindsight. Mechanism: the premise 'low velocity = bad year' is FALSE on this log — 2020 (mult 0.55) and 2021 (mult 0.44, base Sharpe 2.79) were LOW-velocity GOOD years, so the gate throttles winners. This independently confirms and extends FIND-REST-014-b (overlay 'costs sumR, variance-only' on the entry log). NOTE: canonical log ends 2022, so the RQ's '2024 material improvement' claim is untestable here; the 2021 claim IS testable and FAILS. The original 'predicts 3/4 bad years' signal was on the smaller v014 n=119 log and does not replicate. Do NOT wire as a live size gate.

title: Signed-velocity variant is a pure variance-control lever (halves maxDD) with negligible Sharpe gain — not alpha
body: The signed (directional) velocity formulation — cut size when the rate spread is narrowing — is the only variant that does not hurt Sharpe: gated 0.6784 vs base 0.6702 (dSharpe +0.008) while maxDD improves -0.0967 -> -0.0388. But avg_mult is 0.515: it removes roughly half the notional on average. Sharpe is scale-invariant, so a flat Sharpe at half exposure means the DD reduction is mechanical de-risking, not timing alpha. Logged only as an optional maxDD/variance throttle for future risk work, NOT as an edge. Per CLAUDE.md tenet 1 this is a risk-control candidate at best, not a return driver.

title: CB-blackout gate (3-14d before BOE/FED) CONFIRMED on clean calendar — wire as veto (pending Colin approval)
body: Re-ran HYP-039's CB-blackout idea on a CLEAN reconstructed BOE-MPC+FOMC schedule (2025 portion matches cb_calendar.py) against N=101 GBPUSD London ICT trades (2023-08..2025-03). Vetoing entries 3-14 calendar days before the next BOE or FED decision removes 26/101 trades whose avgR=-0.58 (WR 7.7%). Post-gate Sharpe 0.306 vs baseline 0.176 (dSharpe +0.130), avgR +0.41R, sumR +41->+56 (the gate ADDS return, doesn't just de-risk). MC1 permutation p=0.005 (vetoed bucket significantly worse than a random 26-subset). MC2 bootstrap dSharpe 95%CI [+0.048,+0.218], P(>0)=0.999. MC3 prop-challenge pass-rate +19.5pp (63.3%->82.8%). Robust in BOTH sub-periods (+0.104 / +0.165) and across window-bound choices. Verdict: WIRE HARD VETO per RQ-REST-010 rule (dSharpe>0.10, p<0.05, CI>0). Live logic change => requires Colin's logged approval (NON-NEGOTIABLE #4).

title: cb_decisions.json contamination is TOTAL (97%), not a 2023-24 patch problem — wrong FRED series at the root
body: Full audit of data/cache/cb_decisions.json (1192 records, 2014-2026). 1152/1192 = 97% are phantom: |actual_change_bps| is NOT a multiple of 25 (real G10 policy moves are always 25/50/75). Per bank phantom rate: FED 94/94=100%, BOE 465/482=96%, BOJ 87/87=100%, BOC 129/130=99%, RBA 127/128=99%, RBNZ 129/131=98%, SNB 116/117=99%; ECB is the only semi-clean bank (5/23=22% phantom). Signatures: every FED row is dated the 1st of the month (94/94) with random sub-25bps values — there are ZERO real FOMC dates in the file. BOE real decisions DO exist (17, and the 2022-2025 ones are correct) but are buried in 465 near-daily +/-1-2bps noise rows. ROOT CAUSE: scripts/build_cb_decisions.py reads FRED EFFECTIVE/INTERBANK monthly series (FEDFUNDS effective, IR3TIB01* 3-mo interbank, IUDSOIA SONIA) and flags any month with a >=1bp drift as a 'decision'. Monthly series -> first-of-month dates; effective rates wiggle -> phantom changes; wrong series -> not policy-target decisions at all. Should read DFEDTARU/DFEDTARL (Fed target band) and BoE Bank Rate, not effective rates.

title: RQ-REST-015 is NOT blocked on rebuilding cb_decisions.json — wire the blackout veto via cb_calendar.py (already clean)
body: The HYP-061 blackout veto is purely DATE-BASED (3-14 calendar days before next BOE/FED meeting); it needs meeting DATES, not the surprise magnitudes in cb_decisions.json. sovereign/forex/cb_calendar.py already holds a hand-verified clean meeting calendar (CB_MEETINGS, 'Verified against official CB calendars 2026-04-24') with the exact helper is_in_blackout_period() / get_days_to_next_meeting(). sovereign/present_state.py ALREADY imports and uses it correctly — proof the clean path works. Wire ict-engine/orchestrator.py to cb_calendar.is_in_blackout_period(bank, as_of, blackout window mapped to the validated 3-14d) and DO NOT read cb_decisions.json for the gate. CAVEATS: (1) cb_calendar.py only covers 2025-2026; the HYP-061 backtest spans 2023-08..2025-03, so for backtest-reproducibility extend CB_MEETINGS back to 2023 using the clean FOMC+BOE date lists ALREADY hardcoded in scripts/rq_rest_010_cb_blackout.py (lines ~20-26). title: ROOT CAUSE of COMMITMENT_SCORE_STUCK: computed CommitmentState is discarded, never logged — detector is NOT failing
body: The recurring oracle_pulse YELLOW 'COMMITMENT_SCORE_STUCK / Commitment detector failed' (open since 06-12) is MISDIAGNOSED. The CommitmentDetector works correctly; the defect is plumbing. Two log paths both emit a degenerate value: (1) ict/pipeline.py logs commitment_score=_commit_size_mult, but _commit_size_mult = getattr(_commit,'size_multiplier',1.0) if _commit is not None else 1.0, and the orchestrator ALWAYS calls the pipeline with commitment_result=None (ict-engine/orchestrator.py L287/L295, by design — component scores don't exist until the pipeline runs), so _commit is always None inside the pipeline and the logged value is the constant 1.0. (2) orchestrator._log_decisions (L341) hardcodes commitment_score=None. The orchestrator DOES compute the real commitment as a post-pass (_compute_commitment, L306) and uses commit.label to veto UNCOMMITTED signals — then THROWS THE STATE AWAY without logging commit.score. EMPIRICAL CONFIRMATION: data/decision_logs/decisions_2026_06.jsonl = 1774/1797 entries commitment_score=1.0, 23 = None, ZERO carry the computed value. The feature is constant => zero information.

title: IMPACT: commitment_score is a dead feature in the Oracle decision-logger pipeline (silent data loss)
body: CLAUDE.md DECISION_LOGGER pattern lists commitment_score as one of the four captured decision features (commitment, rate differential, library match, bars-since-signal) that Oracle/reflect_cycle reads to learn. Because the logged column is a constant (1.0), Oracle can never learn anything keyed on commitment and any model ingesting it sees a degenerate feature. This is the same class of silent-data-loss the CLAUDE.md Oracle-loop note warns about for missing update_outcome(). SECONDARY DEFECT: the field is also MISLABELED — _commit_size_mult is the size MULTIPLIER (discrete {0.0,0.5,0.75,1.0}), not the commitment SCORE (continuous 0–1). The correct feature to log is commit.score; size_multiplier should be a separate field if wanted. FIX (code change, NON-NEGOTIABLE #4 — Colin approval + param_change_log): in orchestrator._evaluate_pair capture the computed `commit` per surviving ICTSignal and thread commit.score into _log_decisions → log_ict_decision(..., commitment_score=commit.score); stop logging the always-1.0 _commit_size_mult as commitment_score in pipeline.py (rename to commitment_size_mult or drop). Single source of truth = the orchestrator's computed state. Queued as RQ-REST-018. Pulse rule text ('check ict.pipeline for Commitment detector failed') should also be corrected — it points the wrong way.

title: Trailing-stop drag is ~86% a 3-5 day whipsaw cluster; trailing exits surviving >10d are net POSITIVE
body: No-network forensic on the same 318-trade costed 4-pair log (RQ-REST-013 full re-sim is network-blocked tonight: Yahoo 403). Decomposed the -49.0R trailing_stop cluster (HYP-059) by entry-known and exit-known cuts. KEY: by hold-days bucket the drag is overwhelmingly concentrated in SHORT holds -- 3-5d trailing exits = n36, WR 8.3%, meanR -1.17, sumR -42.2R (86% of the entire drag); 0-2d = n7, WR 0%, -10.3R; 6-10d ~flat -3.6R; and 11d+ trailing exits are net POSITIVE +7.2R (WR 44.8%). I.e. the trailing stop only bleeds when it fires early -- when a position survives past ~10 days the trailing exit is fine. Direction is NOT a gate (long -26.1R vs short -22.9R, CIs overlap). Quarter: Q3 worst (-25.3R, summer chop), Q2 ~flat; quarter is entry-known and could be a weak secondary gate. Magnitude: broad bleed not tail blow-ups (72 losers / 25 winners; worst-5 only 24.6% of drag; median trailing exit -0.91R) -> mechanical whipsaw, not bad luck. Reconciles exactly with RQ-REST-012 trailing sumR -48.97R.

title: Sharpened RQ-REST-013 spec: test a trailing-stop ACTIVATION DELAY (time-floor ~5-6d) FIRST
body: The 3-5d concentration converts the full RQ-REST-013 re-sim from a broad grid (TP 2-4R x SL x trailing 1.5/2.0x ATR) into a prioritized first test. Because hold_days is only known at exit it is NOT itself a selectable filter, BUT a rule that PREVENTS the trailing stop from being the active exit during days 0-~5 (wide fixed SL early, trailing engages only after a time-floor) is fully ex-ante implementable and directly targets the -42R cluster while preserving the +7R of mature trailing exits. Recommended re-sim arms, in order: (1) trailing-activation delay of 5 and 6 days [primary]; (2) fixed time-N hold vs trailing [HYP-059 baseline contrast]; (3) widened trailing 1.5x/2.0x ATR [least targeted]. Compare net Sharpe/sumR/maxDD per year, focus flat years 2018/2019. STILL requires network for price paths + Colin approval before any live wiring (NON-NEGOTIABLE #4).

title: No clean ENTRY-known proxy for the 3-5d trailing whipsaw cluster; Tuesday 'edge' is a confounded EURUSD artifact
body: Answered the open question from FIND-REST-018-a/b: hold_days (where the -42.2R whipsaw lives) is exit-known, so is there an ENTRY-known feature that proxies it? Tested all entry-known gates on the FULL 318-trade costed 4-pair log (correct ex-ante framing: veto whole trades across all exit types, not just trailing). Baseline portfolio +95.3R, meanR +0.300, per-trade Sharpe 0.176, WR 45.3%. RESULTS: (1) DOM gates are DEGENERATE -- 95% of entries fall on days 2-6 (month-start signal artifact; day 3 alone=112 trades), so day-of-month buckets are unusable AND day-of-week is confounded with calendar position. (2) The only significant gate, veto_Tue (perm p=0.023, walk-forward sign-consistent), is a MIRAGE: Tuesday badness is EURUSD-concentrated (EURUSD Tue meanR -0.292, AUDUSD -0.067, but GBPUSD +0.309 and USDJPY +0.271 are fine), only 13% of Tuesday trades are the 3-5d whipsaw (captures ~40% of the -42R cluster), and vetoing all 118 Tuesdays REDUCES total R by removing +62R of winners to kill -17R of losers. Under multiple-comparison correction (9 gates) nothing survives BH. VERDICT: there is no clean entry-gate shortcut. This strongly CORROBORATES HYP-059's core thesis -- the regime-fragility/whipsaw lives in the EXIT mechanism, not in entry selection. The fix remains the trailing-ACTIVATION-DELAY re-sim (FIND-REST-018-b), which needs OHLC price paths => still network-blocked. title: 4-pair macro edge is robust to transaction-cost error: break-even ~12.5x spread (point), ~5.6x (95% significance)
body: Cost-stress on the 318-trade costed 4-pair log (2015-2022), model net_R=(pnl-k*spread+swap)/risk, k=1=live (reproduces baseline meanR=0.2998, Sharpe=0.176, sumR=95.35). Decomposition: gross price edge +104.5R; spread drags only -8.3R (8% of gross) at live cost; carry/swap is ~neutral (-0.79R net over this macro-swing sample). Portfolio break-even multiple k*=12.5x (spread must rise ~12x before net sumR hits 0). Bootstrap (5000x): meanR 95% CI at live cost [0.112,0.491]; edge stays SIGNIFICANTLY>0 up to k*=5.6x modeled spread. CONCLUSION: the thin per-trade Sharpe is NOT a cost-modeling artifact, and execution/spread cost is not where this strategy dies. A 5x spread blowup (crisis widening, worse broker, or ~+8 pips slippage on EURUSD round-trip) still leaves a significant edge. Reinforces that the binding lever is the EXIT mechanism (HYP-059 trailing whipsaw), not execution quality. Artifact data/agent/rq_rest_020_cost_stress_results.json.

title: Cost-fragility tracks regime-fragility, not an independent risk; AUDUSD is the cost-fragile pair, GBPUSD the most robust
body: Per-pair break-even spread multiple: GBPUSD 23.1x (51 pips, biggest contributor +38.8R, most robust), USDJPY 20.6x (25 pips), EURUSD 9.6x (19 pips), AUDUSD 6.4x (15 pips, most cost-fragile) -- AUDUSD watch-list for live broker/prop spread, consistent with it being the weakest leg. Per-year break-even multiple maps ONTO the known walk-forward regime story: the cost-fragile years are exactly the flat/non-trending years (2018 k*=1.8x -- merely doubling spread kills 2018; 2019 k*=3.3x), while trending years tolerate huge cost error (2016 18x, 2020 20x, 2022 19x). So cost-fragility and regime-fragility share one root cause (thin edge in non-trending rate regimes); transaction cost is NOT a second, independent failure mode. Actionable: when selecting a live broker/prop spread, the edge tolerates up to ~5x modeled spread before losing significance; prioritize tight execution on AUDUSD/EURUSD; GBPUSD/USDJPY have wide margin.

title: Multiple-testing audit: live portfolio survives BH-FDR(5%); only HYP-059 survives Bonferroni. Program is not a false-discovery field.
body: Offline meta-audit while RQ-REST-013 exit re-sim stays network-blocked (Yahoo 403 reconfirmed this cycle). Built the family of N=26 recorded significance tests across the ledger (one primary p per hypothesis, OOS-preferred). Benjamini-Hochberg FDR at 5%: kmax=12 survive. Among CONFIRMED/VALID edges the FDR survivors are HYP-059 (p=5e-4), HYP-046a/b/c (p=0.002), HYP-045 (0.0033), OVERNIGHT-QQQ (0.0049), HYP-061 (0.005), HYP-049 (0.008) -- i.e. the entire live 4-pair portfolio + the CB-blackout veto + the exit-mechanism finding clear FDR control. GOOD NEWS: the confirmations cluster on the deployed edges, not noise. STRICT VIEW (Bonferroni, FWER, alpha/N=0.00192): only HYP-059 (time>trailing, p=5e-4) survives among deployable edges; HYP-045/046/049/061 do NOT clear Bonferroni. CAVEAT: N=26 is a FLOOR -- true trial count (47 ledger entries + sub-variants + the pre-cost fast-backtester era) is larger, so the real correction is stricter still. CAVEAT2: HYP-046a/b/c are 3 correlated holds of one EUR/GBP edge, inflating both N and the pass count. Bottom line: the most statistically robust single result we own is that the trailing stop is the leak (HYP-059), which independently re-confirms RQ-REST-013 (exit re-sim) as the top lever.

title: HYP-056 is labelled CONFIRMED but fails its own significance test -- recommend relabel to MARGINAL/PARTIAL.

It fails BH and Bonferroni and is not significant even uncorrected on the full-period/OOS-permutation test. The CONFIRMED label overstates the evidence relative to the hypothesis's own caveats. Recommend status -> MARGINAL (or PARTIAL_CONFIRMATION) to keep the ledger honest. No deployment depends on it (test_needed says do-not-implement), so this is a labelling/integrity fix, not a live-risk issue.

title: HYP-059 trailing-stop drag REPLICATES on the independent canonical v015 proof set (411 trades 2015-2024); 3-5d whipsaw cluster = 95% of the drag.
body: Cross-validated REST-018/HYP-059 on data/proof/backtest_trades_v015_2015_2024.csv -- a separately-built tracked snapshot, NOT the logs/forex_backtest_trades.json HYP-059 was confirmed on. Trailing exits n=118, sumR -60.8, meanR -0.515, sharpe -0.335 -- near-identical to HYP-059's costed-log trailing (meanR -0.505, sharpe -0.33). Hold-bucket cut: 3-5d n=45 sumR -58.0 (95%% of the -60.8R trailing drag) meanR -1.289 95%% CI [-1.522,-1.026] -- robustly negative, not a 1-2 trade artifact; 11d+ n=35 sumR +16.2 meanR +0.464 wr 51%% (winners run, replicates). TIME-FLOOR upper bound (forbid trailing exit before day F; book-level, all 411 trades, baseline sumR 156.4 sharpe 0.223): floor_5d removes 39 sub-floor trailing exits (sumR -50.5) -> book sumR 206.9 sharpe 0.332 (+0.109); floor_6d -> sharpe 0.384 (+0.161). title: cb_decisions.json is 94% phantom -- but the 25bps entry gate already neutralizes it for entry_engine. RQ-REST-017's 'entry_engine consuming 97% phantom' framing is INACCURATE for the gated paths.
body: ROOT CAUSE: scripts/build_cb_decisions.py manufactures a 'decision' from every diff of a FRED rate-LEVEL series above MIN_CHANGE_BPS=1. FRED policy-rate series are effective/averaged rates that wobble sub-decision, so the file holds 1192 rows for 8 banks over 2014-2026 where only ~50-70 real rate-change decisions exist. Magnitude profile: |actual|>=12.5bps (plausibly real) = 216 (18.1%); 0<|actual|<12.5 (phantom wiggle) = 976 (81.9%); the +/-1bp bucket alone = 572 rows (48%). 60.3% of rows are stamped to day-01 of a month (fabricated date); BOE is daily-sampled so it alone has 482 'decisions' (~40/yr vs 8 real meetings/yr). CORRECTION TO RQ-REST-017: entry_engine.CBEventTrigger.check() and check_historical() both filter abs(surprise_bps) < CB_MIN_SURPRISE_BPS(=25). Only 70/1192 rows (5.9%) survive that gate and 69 of the 70 have |actual|>=12.5bps. So Edge-3 / HYP-061 entry signals are NOT consuming phantom magnitudes -- the 25bps gate protects them. The '97% phantom' is true of the FILE, false of the entry path.

title: The REAL residual risks: (1) ungated consumer harvest_daily_panel.collect_positioning() reports a phantom as 'latest CB decision'; (2) ALL surviving rows carry fabricated DATES, and cb_calendar.CB_MEETINGS (the correct real-date source) holds only 1 date.
body: (1) harvest_daily_panel.collect_positioning() takes d[-1] with NO magnitude gate and labels it 'latest' CB decision meta. d[-1] today = {2026-04-01 RBNZ +5bps surprise} -- a phantom (RBNZ has no Apr-1 meeting; 5bps is sub-decision). Any oracle/reader of the positioning panel is being fed a fake 'latest central bank action'. This is the actual live corruption surface, not the gated entry signals. (2) Even the 70 gated rows have FABRICATED dates (day-01 monthly stamp or daily-wobble dates), not real meeting dates. So Edge-3's 5-business-day post-decision entry window and HYP-061's 3-14d pre-BOE/FED blackout are date-anchored to non-meeting days when run off this file. HYP-061's CONFIRMATION is intact -- it was tested on a separately reconstructed BOE+FOMC schedule, not this file -- but its live WIRING (RQ-REST-015) needs a real meeting-date table at runtime. That table, sovereign/forex/cb_calendar.py CB_MEETINGS, currently contains only ONE date (2026-04-24). CONCLUSION: RQ-REST-016 (back-extend CB_MEETINGS to real scheduled dates) is the true unblock-everything prerequisite -- it is both the fix for RQ-REST-015 wiring AND the correct rebuild source for cb_decisions.json. Recommended rebuild: anchor decisions to CB_MEETINGS dates and snap actual_change to the nearest 25bps, instead of diffing FRED levels. Rebuild itself needs network (FRED) so it stays queued; this diagnostic is offline-complete.

title: Verified at data level: two decision-logger bugs are real and pervasive -- commitment_score is constant 1.0, and trade_id is absent everywhere (OUTCOME_LOOP_STALL).
body: Read-only audit of data/decision_logs/decisions_2026_05.jsonl and decisions_2026_06.jsonl. (1) commitment_score: 100% of entries carrying the field log exactly 1.0 -- May 1510/1510, June 1788/1788 (25 June rows lack the field). Mechanism confirmed in ict-engine/orchestrator.py: _compute_commitment() builds a `commit` CommitmentState at line 306 and uses it for the UNCOMMITTED veto (line 312), but the log call at line 341 passes commitment_score=None, and sovereign/intelligence/decision_logger.py treats 1.0 as the neutral sentinel -- so the computed score is discarded and 1.0 is persisted. This is exactly RQ-REST-018 / FIND-REST-017-a/b; the fix (retain commit.score and pass it to log_ict_decision) is correct and is a Claude Code task. (2) trade_id: 0 of 3323 entries across both months carry any trade_id, so update_outcome() has no key to match closing trades to entries -- OUTCOME_LOOP_STALL confirmed at schema level (CLAUDE.md NON-NEGOTIABLE #2 broken). Corroborating degeneracy: outcomes are almost entirely EXPIRED (May 1509, June 1794), with only 13 LOSS in June and ZERO recorded WINS -- Oracle is learning from essentially no real closed-loop signal. Both bugs require Claude Code (orchestrator.py + decision_logger wiring); neither is safe to fix from a REST cycle under NON-NEGOTIABLE #4.

summary: OUTCOME_LOOP_STALL root cause is DATA POLLUTION, not just missing trade_id. The live decision log data/decision_logs/decisions_2026_06.jsonl holds 1919 records whose entry_timestamps span 2023-08..2026-06 (240/571/569/539 per yr) -- these are BACKTEST replays. log_ict_decision (ict/pipeline.py:663) has NO live/backtest gate, so run_ict_backtest/permutation_test_ict/validate_signals_retrospective/optimize_ict_stops all write into the live log. Backtest entries dated 2023-25 can never be closed (update_outcome derives month from entry_timestamp -> looks for decisions_2023_08.jsonl which does not exist) -> stuck OPEN -> swept EXPIRED. Result: ICT 1892 EXPIRED/1 LOSS, FOREX 12 LOSS/8 EXPIRED/5 OPEN; Oracle trains on ~13 rows.
evidence: 1919 rows, trade_id present 0/1919; year hist 240/571/569/539; only decisions_2026_05/06.jsonl exist

summary: The URGENT OUTCOME_LOOP_STALL alarm is a STEADY-STATE FALSE POSITIVE on the FOREX/OANDA path — the forex loop IS closing. pulse_check._backfill_decision_outcomes re-fetches OANDA's last-100 closed trades every 2h and fires the URGENT alarm whenever n_backfilled==0. But n_backfilled==0 is the NORMAL steady state: run_oanda_paper.py's TradeTracker already closes trades at close time via update_outcome (in-memory _open dict), so by the 2h backfill the matching decision records are already outcome!=None (not OPEN) and correctly match nothing. Alarm condition (n_attempted>0 and n_backfilled==0) cannot distinguish 'already closed (healthy)' from 'never matched (broken)'.
evidence: OANDA-native FOREX decision records carry real outcomes: tid25 AUD_NZD LOSS, tid29 USD_JPY WIN, tid59/119 AUD_NZD LOSS, tid123 USD_JPY WIN, tid134/144/154/165 USD_CAD LOSS (decisions_2026_06.jsonl). Only OPEN forex records are yfinance scan signals (GBPUSD=X/AUDUSD=X/EURUSD=X) on pairs the live OANDA book doesn't trade.
action: Fix alarm logic: fire only when a closed OANDA trade's matching decision record is still outcome==None OR absent. Folded into RQ-REST-019b.

summary: FOREX outcome matching ignores the trade_id join key that ALREADY exists on both sides. update_outcome() matches on pair+entry_timestamp(+system) only; trade_id is not even a parameter. Yet oanda_fills.jsonl is keyed by trade_id (6,25,29,47,51,...,177) and the OANDA-native decision records store trade_id/order_id (tid25->oid24 ... tid177->oid176). So a handful of closed fills on pairs with no same-day OPEN record fall through with no closed-loop outcome, and matching leans on the fragile Tier-2 same-UTC-date+FIFO fallback instead of an exact id join.
evidence: Unmatched fills: tid47/93 AUD_USD, tid51/83 GBP_USD, tid72/105 EUR_USD (oanda_fills.jsonl) have no OANDA-native decision record; the only same-normalized-pair OPEN records are yfinance scan signals on non-matching dates (GBPUSD=X 06-09/06-12, AUDUSD=X 06-16/06-17, EURUSD=X 06-23).
action: REST-030 spec Change 2 (broker_trade_id join) is the right fix; NOTE the forex data to implement it is ALREADY present — only update_outcome + the backfill need to consume trade_id. Reinforces RQ-REST-019b.

summary: CORRECTS FIND-REST-031-b. The trade_id/order_id join key does NOT exist on the gated-entry side. Of 1922 Jun+Jul decision records written by the real signal path (non-empty signal_layers_active, source!=fills_backfill), 0 carry trade_id and 0 carry order_id. The only 11 records in the whole Jun+Jul log that carry a trade_id are ALL source=fills_backfill reconstructed stubs. So update_outcome() has nothing to id-join a genuinely gated live record to its OANDA exit; the id only ever appears AFTER the reconstruction stub is created.
evidence: decisions_2026_06+07.jsonl: gated=1922 (trade_id=0, order_id=0, outcome!=None=1915, outcome=None=7); fills_backfill stubs=11 (all carry trade_id).

summary: The real (non-false-positive) outcome-loop gap is narrow and now enumerated. 7 gated records rot at outcome=None (4 FOREX + 3 ICT; the 3 ICT are the 2026-07-01 GBPUSD NY_Open entries). title: Price-path exit re-sim (offline) independently REPLICATES the HYP-059 resolution — no new exit lever
summary: RQ-REST-013 was ERROR/DRY-RUN since 2026-05-18, blocked on network for a full price-path exit re-sim. Network no longer needed: cached daily OHLC for all 4 live pairs (2014-2026) exists at data/research/positioning_family/spot_cache/*_ohlc.parquet. Ran the re-sim holding the recorded v015 entries fixed (412-trade proof set) and varying only the exit: fixed time-N (5/8/10/15/20), stop-only, immediate 1.25x-ATR trail, trail-activation-delay 5d/6d, and wider trails 1.5x/2.0x ATR.
method: R = signed_return / (K_STOP*ATR_entry/entry), K_STOP=2.0 (calibrated off the 9 recorded 'stop' exits). Deterministic policies exclude the signal-driven 'reversal' exit (79 trades) which cannot be reproduced without the entry engine.

results.recon_faithfulness: corr(recorded,reconstructed)=0.60; a single ~2.90x R-normalization constant reconciles reconstruction to recorded across the time/trailing/stop buckets (time 74.4->206.1, trailing -23.7->-60.8, stop -6.4->-16.9) => re-sim is faithful up to R-scaling; RELATIVE arm ranking is valid.
results.arm_ranking_reconSumR: trail_wide_2.0 57.6 > wide_1.5 47.8 > trail_delay6 41.7 ~ delay5 41.4 > time10 39.8 ~ stop_only 38.8 > trail_immediate 35.9 > time5 29.4
results.flat_year_2022_inversion: recorded +36.09R (biggest year) vs EVERY deterministic arm NEGATIVE (-5..-12R). The 2022 edge is signal/reversal-driven, NOT exit-mechanical.
results.reversal_bucket: reversal exits contribute +27.99R recorded that no trail/time policy reproduces (recon -8.4R) -> the signal-driven reversal exit is load-bearing, not removable.

verdict: CONVERGENT / NO NEW LEVER. Findings match the 2026-07-01 ExitConfig 180-config sweep (0/180 beat v015 in both regimes, 0/180 FDR-sig; 'a moderate 1.5x trail maximizes return AND Sharpe'): (a) wider trail (1.5-2.0x) ranks best on return+Sharpe here too; (b) the per-trade trailing 'drag' does NOT convert to a portfolio A/B gain (my deterministic arms reproduce only ~1/2.9 of recorded sumR and invert 2022). This is an INDEPENDENT REPLICATION (price-path re-sim vs the sweep's proof-set replay), NOT new evidence to reopen HYP-059. v015 exit remains optimal.
action: RQ-REST-013 -> DONE (converged). Do not re-queue trailing-drag exit experiments without genuinely new data.

title: OUTCOME_LOOP_STALL pulse is a FALSE POSITIVE — detector re-counts already-resolved historical OANDA closes every cycle; the real win/loss loop is functioning
summary: Read-only audit of the recurring URGENT OUTCOME_LOOP_STALL alarm (fired every 2h since ~07-07, count now '23 closed, 0 matched' up from 22). Root cause is in sovereign/oracle/pulse_check.py backfill_closed_outcomes(): each pulse re-fetches OandaBridge().get_closed_trades(limit=100) and calls decision_logger.update_outcome() for every closed trade with a sane stop. update_outcome only matches decision records where outcome is None (still OPEN). Any close already back-filled in a prior pulse (or that never had a decision record) can therefore NEVER re-match, yet still increments n_attempted on every subsequent pulse. Once all sane-stop closes are resolved, every future pulse reports 'N closed, 0 matched' -> perpetual URGENT alarm with no new failure. The climbing count (22->23) during a 9-day fill drought is the signature: n_attempted accumulates historical closes while n_backfilled stays 0 because nothing new is OPEN.
method: Traced pulse_check.py:582-645 (backfill_closed_outcomes) + decision_logger.update_outcome/_norm_pair. Cross-checked decisions_2026_07.jsonl: 58 rows = 55 ICT EXPIRED (paper limit orders never hit = correct) + 2 FOREX LOSS (matched: USD_JPY -1.0 via fills_backfill 07-01; USDJPY=X -1.002 07-03) + 1 FOREX EURUSD=X outcome=None. The one None is the single still-OPEN trade (equity_curve open_trade_count=1) -> correct, not a miss. _norm_pair collapses USDJPY=X/USD_JPY/USDJPY equal, so venue-format mismatch is NOT the cause (the 07-03 USDJPY=X<->USD_JPY match proves it).

results.loop_functioning: 2/2 genuinely-closed FOREX decisions matched+backfilled in July; 1 open trade correctly None
